Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The laboratory results show a mildly elevated HbA1c (6.1%), suggesting impaired glucose tolerance or early diabetes, while fasting glucose is within normal limits. Renal function, electrolytes, and lipid profile are within normal ranges, with borderline triglycerides. No acute abnormalities detected; recommend clinical correlation for glycemic control and possible follow-up for diabetes risk.
